byKHQA Staff

Quincy, Ill. (KHQA) —On Monday afternoon, August 19, the Quincy Police Department held their “Tip A Cop” event at the Texas Roadhouse to raise money for the Special Olympics.

The QPD worked alongside Texas Roadhouse staff to raise money and awareness for Special Olympics Illinois. All customers had to do was simply leave a donation at their table with 100% of the proceeds benefitting Special Olympics Illinois.

"It's hard to put into words.Without this money, a lot of these athletes wouldn't be able to participate because it costs a lot for the travel, and the rental of places to do it," said Officer Amber Haistings with the Quincy Police Department.

If you we're not able to make it to the event but would like to support QPD's efforts for Special Olympics, visit their Facebook pageherefor contact information.

2025Sinclair, Inc.
title: Quincy Police raise funds for Special Olympics at Texas Roadhouse event
description: On Monday afternoon, the Quincy Police Department held their “Tip A Cop” event at the Texas Roadhouse to raise money for the Special Olympics.
